job_description:
We are:
The Advanced Technology Centers (ATCs) is the engine for reinvention in our clients’ transformation journey. Powered by more than 255,000* people across 24 countries, ATCs provide our clients seamless access to industry insights and innovative technology solutions.



Stronger together!

The Advanced Technology Centers (ATCs) make a tremendous impact in solving our clients’ business problems by leveraging Innovation, Intelligence, Industry insights, new IT, and new technology skills. Now, with the global environment changing at a faster pace, our clients are facing unprecedented challenges, and they need us more than ever before. As a Network, ATCs are positioned to unlock greater opportunities and exponential value for our clients.



The value for our clients and our people:
For our clients, the Network provides the strength of our geographic diversity, greater resilience, and seamless access to the deepest industry knowledge, the latest in Gen AI solutions, and tech expertise from around the world. For our people, it brings an opportunity to shape truly boundaryless career paths in a highly collaborative team of experts where they can learn from each other and solve the world’s most complex client challenges.



You are:
A DevOps Engineer, you will design, implement, and manage scalable, secure, and efficient DevOps pipelines and cloud infrastructure. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to automate workflows, ensure system reliability, and integrate security into the DevOps lifecycle. Leveraging tools such as Jenkins, Azure DevOps, Kubernetes, Terraform, and Prometheus, you will drive continuous integration, delivery, and monitoring of applications.



Your strong technical expertise in cloud platforms, containerization, CI/CD automation, and infrastructure as code will be essential to deliver impactful solutions.



The Work:
As a DevOps Engineer, your primary responsibility will be to design and implement scalable DevOps pipelines and manage cloud infrastructure across platforms like AWS, Azure, or GCP. You will automate workflows using tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and Terraform, ensuring system reliability, security, and observability.



This role involves collaborating with engineering teams to address infrastructure requirements, troubleshoot issues, and optimize workflows for scalability and efficiency. Proficiency in scripting languages like Python, Bash, or PowerShell, along with expertise in containerization and orchestration, will be critical to succeed in this role.



This role will require a minimum of 3 days per week in the office. Hiring locations: Tampa or Columbus.


What you need:

 * A minimum of three years of experience working with at least one major cloud platform such as AWS, Azure, or GCP for cloud infrastructure management and deployment.
 * A minimum of one year of experience with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for container orchestration and scaling
 * A minimum of one year of experience Linux fundamentals and scripting languages such as Python, Bash or PowerShell
 * Bachelor's degree or equivalent (minimum 12 years) work experience. (If Associate Degree, must have minimum 6 years work experience)



Bonus points if you have:

 * Strong knowledge in CI/CD tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Harness, AWS CodeDeploy/CodePipeline, Azure DevOps, or similar platforms for automating continuous integration and delivery pipelines.
 * Experience with Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure provisioning and configuration.
 * Familiarity with tools like Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, and Splunk for system monitoring and observability.
 * Experience in creating dashboards for monitoring and reporting using tools such as Grafana, Kibana, Datadog, Splunk, or similar platforms.
 * Knowledge of security and code quality tools such as SonarQube, Checkmarx, and Veracode for integrating security into DevOps workflows.
 * Experience with object-oriented programming (Python, Java)
 * Experience using AI for software development


Compensation at Accenture varies depending on a wide array of factors, which may include but are not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set, and level of experience. As required by local law, Accenture provides a reasonable range of compensation for roles that may be hired as set forth below.
We anticipate this job posting will be posted until 05/02/2026.

Requesting an Accommodation

Accenture is committed to providing equal employment opportunities for persons with disabilities or religious observances, including reasonable accommodation when needed. If you are hired by Accenture and require accommodation to perform the essential functions of your role, you will be asked to participate in our reasonable accommodation process. Accommodations made to facilitate the recruiting process are not a guarantee of future or continued accommodations once hired.

If you would like to be considered for employment opportunities with Accenture and have accommodation needs such as for a disability or religious observance, please call us toll free at 1 (877) 889-9009 or send us an email or speak with your recruiter.




Equal Employment Opportunity Statement

We believe that no one should be discriminated against because of their differences. All employment decisions shall be made without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by federal, state, or local law. Our rich diversity makes us more innovative, more competitive, and more creative, which helps us better serve our clients and our communities.

Other Employment Statements

Applicants for employment in the US must have work authorization that does not now or in the future require sponsorship of a visa for employment authorization in the United States.

Candidates who are currently employed by a client of Accenture or an affiliated Accenture business may not be eligible for consideration.

Job candidates will not be obligated to disclose sealed or expunged records of conviction or arrest as part of the hiring process. Further, at Accenture a criminal conviction history is not an absolute bar to employment.

The Company will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. Additionally, employees who have access to the compensation information of other employees or applicants as a part of their essential job functions cannot disclose the pay of other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise have access to compensation information, unless the disclosure is (a) in response to a formal complaint or charge, (b) in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted by the employer, or (c) consistent with the Company's legal duty to furnish information.

California requires additional notifications for applicants and employees. If you are a California resident, live in or plan to work from Los Angeles County upon being hired for this position, please click here for additional important information.

Please read Accenture’s Recruiting and Hiring Statement for more information on how we process your data during the Recruiting and Hiring process.

Valorem Reply is an award-winning digital transformation firm focused on delivering solutions around data-driven enterprise, IT modernization, customer experience, product transformation and digital workplace by leveraging the power of Microsoft technologies. We provide hyper-scale and agile delivery of unique digital business services, strategic business models and design-led user experiences. Our innovative strategies and solutions securely and rapidly transform the way our clients do business.


We are hiring a DevOps Engineer to design, automate, and scale cloud-native infrastructure supporting enterprise-grade applications. This role will own Kubernetes environments, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud platform operations in Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The ideal candidate brings strong automation discipline and experience operating production-grade distributed systems.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 * Design, deploy, and operate Kubernetes clusters in production environments
 * Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines to support secure and repeatable deployments
 * Implement and manage service mesh architectures using Istio
 * Administer and optimize Google Cloud Platform infrastructure
 * Integrate Jira workflows into development and release processes
 * Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve reliability, performance, and security
 * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement infrastructure as code and automation standards
 * Additional duties as required


MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS

 * 3 years of experience in DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or Infrastructure Engineering
 * 2 years of hands-on Kubernetes administration in production environments
 * 2 years of experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines
 * 2 years of experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
 * 2 years of experience implementing and managing Istio service mesh
 * Experience administering Jira for engineering workflow management


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 * Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools (Terraform or similar)
 * Experience with container security and vulnerability management
 * Experience supporting microservices-based architectures
 * Google Cloud Professional certification
 * Experience operating highly available distributed systems

About Reply


Reply specializes in the design and implementation of solutions based on new communication channels and digital media. Reply is a network of highly specialized companies supporting global industrial groups operating in the telecom and media, industry and services, banking, insurance and public administration sectors in the definition and development of business models enabled for the new paradigms of AI, cloud computing, digital media and the Internet of Things. Reply services include Consulting, System Integration and Digital Services.


Reply is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to provide equal opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. If you need assistance and reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, email us at jobusa@reply.com. Visit our website at www.reply.com to learn more about our open roles.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

We Are

The beginning of a new Data & AI decade that will reshape work and society has begun. Accenture is stepping boldly into this future with a clear strategy and purpose: to help clients optimize and reinvent their business with data & AI — backed by a $3B investment and commitment to our people to do industry-defining work.

With over 45,000 professionals dedicated to Data & AI, Accenture’s Data & AI organization brings together our Experienced Innovation, Strategic Investment, Exceptional Talent, and Power Ecosystem.



You Are

As an Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Computational Science professional, you will play a pivotal role in formulating real-world problems into practical, efficient, and scalable AI and Machine Learning solutions. You will be responsible for developing and implementing cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions that drive innovation and enhance performance. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams and leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and data analysis to solve complex problems and deliver impactful AI-driven solutions.



The Work

We are seeking a highly motivated Agentic DevOps Engineer to support our Agentic DevOps initiatives. This role is ideal for a hands-on technical leader ready to contribute to the development and deployment of scalable, production-ready Generative AI agentic systems. You will work closely with senior leadership and cross-functional teams to operationalize reusable DevOps frameworks and ensure seamless delivery of agentic applications across cloud environments.



Key Responsibilities

 * Assist in implementing scalable DevOps frameworks for agentic systems using LangGraph, Crew AI, Autogen, and other orchestration tools.

 * Support the development of reusable components for agent lifecycle management, orchestration, monitoring, and metering.

 * Contribute to CI/CD pipeline setup and maintenance for public and private cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP).

 * Collaborate with engineering and DevOps teams to ensure production readiness, observability, and compliance.

 * Participate in client enablement efforts, including deployment support and onboarding.

 * Maintain documentation and contribute to continuous improvement of agentic DevOps processes.



Travel may be required for this role. The amount of travel will vary from 0 to 100% depending on business need and client requirements.



This role is located in San Diego, CA. Successful applicants must reside or be willing to relocate to San Diego, CA prior to starting.


Here's what you need

 * Minimum of 4 years of experience in DevOps, AI/ML engineering, or cloud-native development.

 * Minimum of 1 year of experience with LLMs, agentic frameworks (LangGraph, Crew AI, Autogen), and prompt engineering, RAG.

 * Minimum of 4 years of experience with CI/CD tools, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform).

 * Minimum of 3 years’ experience in deploying solutions on Azure.

 * Minimum of 1 year of experience in deploying and monitoring Generative AI systems in production environments.

 * Minimum of 3 years of working on cloud security, observability, and performance optimization.



Bonus points if you have

 * Experience with multi-agent orchestration.

 * Exposure to enterprise data platforms (e.g., SAP, SharePoint).

 * Certifications in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP, Oracle)

 * Experience in life sciences, fintech, or regulated industries is a plus.

BECOME A PART OF OUR CARING COMMUNITY


The Associate Director of DevOps Engineering is an exciting opportunity to shape Humana’s DevOps future. In this strategic and technical leadership role, you will guide transformative DevOps initiatives, leading a dynamic team of engineers dedicated to innovation and excellence. By driving standardization and forging strong partnerships with software delivery teams, you will boost the speed, security, and reliability of our software pipeline. This hands-on leader will oversee CI/CD processes, inspire cross-team collaboration, and cultivate a culture of continuous improvement—empowering both engineering and operations teams to deliver outstanding results.



The Associate Director of DevOps will lead a team of DevOps engineers to advance Humana’s DevOps platform, enhance security, and accelerate delivery timelines. This role focuses on developing engineering expertise in cloud and automation technologies, promoting productivity and improving the developer experience. The leader will drive the DevOps strategy, implement automation across the development lifecycle, and foster collaboration between software development and operations teams to enable secure, efficient software delivery.



USE YOUR SKILLS TO MAKE AN IMPACT


Required Qualifications

 * Bachelor's degree in science, engineering, or an equivalent technical field
 * 8-10+ years in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Engineering with 3+ years leadership experience.
 * 3+ years' experience leading engineers implementing CI/CD pipelines using tools like Azure DevOps, Jenkins, GitLab CI, or CircleCI to automate application deployment
 * 2+ years' experience managing all non-engineering functions like recruiting, financial budgets, career development, and more for a large growing engineering team
 * Extensive people leader skills with dedication to grow talent and skills across the organization.
 * Prior experience fostering a culture of collaboration between development and operations teams to promote transparency, shared responsibility, and streamlined processes, resulting in faster change lead times



Preferred Qualifications

 * Master's degree or equivalent experience
 * Expertise with major cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
 * Extensive experience with CI/CD tools, containerization (Docker), orchestration (Kubernetes), and pipeline-as-code
 * Experience with monitoring and logging solutions like Dynatrace, Prometheus, ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana), or Splunk for proactive issue detection and troubleshooting.
 * Must be passionate about contributing to an organization focused on continuously improving consumer experiences



Additional Information

To ensure Home or Hybrid Home/Office employees' ability to work effectively, the self-provided internet service of Home or Hybrid Home/Office employees must meet the following criteria:

At minimum, a download speed of 25 Mbps and an upload speed of 10 Mbps is required; wireless, wired cable or DSL connection is suggested.

Satellite, cellular and microwave connection can be used only if approved by leadership.

Employees who live and work from Home in the state of California, Illinois, Montana, or South Dakota will be provided a bi-weekly payment for their internet expense.

Humana will provide Home or Hybrid Home/Office employees with telephone equipment appropriate to meet the business requirements for their position/job.

Work from a dedicated space lacking ongoing interruptions to protect member PHI / HIPAA information.



Scheduled Weekly Hours



40



Pay Range

The compensation range below reflects a good faith estimate of starting base pay for full time (40 hours per week) employment at the time of posting. The pay range may be higher or lower based on geographic location and individual pay will vary based on demonstrated job related skills, knowledge, experience, education, certifications, etc.




$142,300 - $195,700 per year




This job is eligible for a bonus incentive plan. This incentive opportunity is based upon company and/or individual performance.



Description of Benefits

Humana, Inc. and its affiliated subsidiaries (collectively, “Humana”) offers competitive benefits that support whole-person well-being. Associate benefits are designed to encourage personal wellness and smart healthcare decisions for you and your family while also knowing your life extends outside of work. Among our benefits, Humana provides medical, dental and vision benefits, 401(k) retirement savings plan, time off (including paid time off, company and personal holidays, volunteer time off, paid parental and caregiver leave), short-term and long-term disability, life insurance and many other opportunities.





ABOUT US


About Humana: Humana Inc. (NYSE: HUM) is a leading U.S. healthcare company. Through our Humana insurance services and our CenterWell healthcare services, we make it easier for the millions of people we serve to achieve their best health – delivering the care and service they need, when they need it. These efforts are leading to a better quality of life for people with Medicare and Medicaid, families, individuals, military service personnel, and communities at large. Learn more about what we offer at Humana.com and at CenterWell.com.





Equal Opportunity Employer



It is the policy of Humana not to discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, marital status, genetic information, disability or protected veteran status. It is also the policy of Humana to take affirmative action, in compliance with Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act and VEVRAA, to employ and to advance in employment individuals with disability or protected veteran status, and to base all employment decisions only on valid job requirements. This policy shall apply to all employment actions, including but not limited to recruitment, hiring, upgrading, promotion, transfer, demotion, layoff, recall, termination, rates of pay or other forms of compensation and selection for training, including apprenticeship, at all levels of employment.

Accenture Flex offers you the flexibility of local fixed-duration project-based work powered by Accenture, a leading global professional services company. Accenture is consistently recognized on FORTUNE's 100 Best Companies to Work For and Diversity Inc's Top 50 Companies For Diversity lists.



As an Accenture Flex employee, you will apply your skills and experience to help drive business transformation for leading organizations and communities. In addition to delivering innovative solutions for Accenture's clients, you will work with a highly skilled, diverse network of people across Accenture businesses who are using the latest emerging technologies to address today's biggest business challenges.



You will receive competitive rewards and access to benefits programs and world-class learning resources. Accenture Flex employees work in their local metro area onsite at the project, significantly reducing and/or eliminating the demands to travel.



Key Responsibilities:

 * Design, implement, and manage CI/CD pipelines across Azure and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) environments

 * Automate build, deployment, and release processes to support cloud-native core banking applications

 * Build and optimize automation scripts (PowerShell, Python, or similar) for environment provisioning, deployment, and operational tasks

 * Build regression and functional testing suites in Postman and integrate them with Newman in Azure Pipelines

 * Integrate DevOps pipelines with API services, event-driven architectures, and microservices

 * Implement and manage environment configuration, version control, and release governance

 * Monitor pipeline performance and troubleshoot build and deployment failures and environmental issues

 * Collaborate with development, architecture, and testing teams to ensure seamless deployment and integration workflows

 * Ensure adherence to security, compliance, and DevSecOps best practices

 * Support cloud infrastructure setup, scaling, and operational stability across Azure and OCI


Basic Qualifications:

 * Minimum of 3 years of experience in DevOps, CI/CD, or cloud engineering roles



Preferred Qualifications:

 * Experience with Postman, REST APIs, and regression and functional API testing

 * Experience with API security and secret management in DevOps pipelines

 * Hands on experience with Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar CI CD tools

 * Experience with Azure cloud services App Services, AKS, Functions, Storage, Networking, etc.

 * Working knowledge of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure OCI services and deployment models

 * Experience implementing CI CD pipelines and automation frameworks

 * Proficiency in at least one scripting language Python preferred

 * Familiarity with containerization technologies Docker, Kubernetes

 * Strong understanding of version control systems Git GitHub

 * Experience in banking or financial services, especially core banking or large scale transformation programs

 * Hands on scripting skills with Python or PowerShell

 * Experience with monitoring and observability tools Azure Monitor, OCI Monitoring, Dynatrace, Splunk, etc.

 * Exposure to DevSecOps practices, including security scanning and compliance automation

 * Familiarity with multi cloud or hybrid cloud architectures Azure OCI

Deloitte Global is the engine of the Deloitte network. Our professionals reach across disciplines and borders to develop and lead global initiatives. We deliver strategic programs and services that unite our organization.

Work you'll do

As the AWS DevOps Engineer, you will play a critical role in building and managing an automated DevOps release pipeline which delivers tooling for next generation application development efforts and on-going production operations. You will embrace and promote Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) mindset, ensuring efficient and reliable processes across the development lifecycle.

Primary Responsibilities:

 * Collaborate with engineers and cloud architects to support development teams with a full set of ALM tools by leading the establishment of the right tooling and processes that will result in a fully automated release management pipeline to include: the automated build process, environment setups, testing scripts, deployments, and production operational metrics/debugging information (to target developers).
 * Partner with development and operations teams to facilitate practical automation solutions and custom modules. Troubleshoot automation issues and when required engage additional resources to find practical solutions that move projects forward.
 * Be a trusted automation and tooling advisor by providing objective, practical and relevant insights and advice.
 * Deliver assignments based on project objectives and support projects to completion. Ensure deliverables are completed within target timeframes and are of high-quality.
 * Support established KPIs to ensure performance is measured against expected business outcomes.


Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship now or at any time in the future

The team

Deloitte Technology works at the forefront of technology development and processes to support and protect Deloitte around the world. In this truly global environment, we operate not in "what is" but rather "what can be" to help Deloitte deliver and connect with its clients, its communities, and one another in ways not previously conceived.

Qualifications

Required:

 * Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, or other technical field, or equivalent experience
 * Strong experience with AWS DevOps, Git, EKS, and Terraform
 * Experience with container and orchestration technologies.


Preferred:

 * AWS DevOps certification

Valorem Reply, part of the Reply Network, is a leader in security-focused digital transformation. We advise and support our clients across cloud security, identity, and automation — enabling them to operate securely and efficiently in a complex digital world.


As a DevOps Engineer, you will deploy and automate identity orchestration flows using Ping Identity’s DaVinci platform. You will help integrate DaVinci workflows into CI/CD pipelines and collaborate with cloud, infrastructure, and security teams to support scalable and secure orchestration in hybrid environments. Your work will help operationalize identity use cases with automation, resilience, and compliance in mind.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 * Implement and maintain identity orchestration flows using Ping DaVinci. Experience customizing or building Ping DaVinci nodes
 * Integrate DaVinci workflows into DevOps toolchains and CI/CD pipelines like GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or Azure DevOps
 * Automate deployment and configuration of DaVinci nodes and identity journeys across environments
 * Collaborate with infrastructure, cloud, and security teams to ensure reliable orchestration at scale
 * Monitor and optimize orchestration flows for performance, resilience, and security compliance
 * Implement version control and promotion strategies for DaVinci flows
 * Provide support on orchestration tooling within the DevOps lifecycle


MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS

 * 3+ years experience in DevOps, IAM engineering, or API platform architecture
 * Experience with Ping Identity’s DaVinci platform
 * Knowledge of identity protocols including SAML, OAuth 2.0, OIDC
 * Proficiency in RESTful APIs and JSON
 * Experience managing infrastructure as code using tools like Terraform, Helm, or Ansible
 * Scripting ability in Bash, PowerShell, Python, or similar


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 * Experience with PingOne Suite including PingOne SSO, Protect, Risk, MFA
 * Strong working knowledge of JavaScript for custom logic and flow extensions
 * Experience working with XML for federation, policy, and configuration artifacts
 * Advanced use of JSON for DaVinci payloads, API integrations, and event handling
 * Basic proficiency with HTML and CSS for custom UI, branding, and hosted page integration
 * Experience debugging browser-based authentication and identity flows
 * Familiarity with Kubernetes and Docker
 * Experience with cloud-native identity architectures in Azure and AWS
 * Certifications in Ping DaVinci, DevOps Engineering, or major cloud platforms

About Reply
Reply specializes in the design and implementation of solutions based on new communication channels and digital media. Reply is a network of highly specialized companies supporting global industrial groups operating in the telecom and media, industry and services, banking, insurance and public administration sectors in the definition and development of business models enabled for the new paradigms of AI, cloud computing, digital media and the Internet of Things. Reply services include Consulting, System Integration and Digital Services.

Summary of Job Description:

The Manager, DevOps Automation is responsible for the strategy, delivery, and ongoing maturity of Panda Restaurant Group’s infrastructure automation and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) capabilities. This position leads a team of DevOps Automation Engineers while serving as a hands‑on technical leader, ensuring enterprise infrastructure platforms are scalable, reliable, secure, and operationally efficient. The Manager, DevOps Automation partners closely with Infrastructure, Cloud, Security, Application, and Operations teams to enable standardized automation across on‑premises, cloud, and hybrid environments. With a strong emphasis on automation tools, orchestration platforms, and automation governance, this position reduces operational toil, accelerates delivery, and improves platform stability. In addition, the Manager provides leadership and oversight for Active Directory and Microsoft 365 (O365) automation and operational excellence, ensuring identity and productivity platforms remain secure and resilient at enterprise scale.




You’ll get a chance to:

 * Leads the design, implementation, and governance of enterprise DevOps automation and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) solutions across data center, cloud, and hybrid environments.
 * Manages a team of DevOps Automation Engineers, coaching and developing associates to elevate their performance and for long‑term growth and succession. Works with leadership to recruit, select, and retain team members.
 * Serves as the primary technical authority for Ansible, including playbook design, role development, collections, and orchestration workflows.
 * Responsible for the platform strategy, configuration, and operational governance of Ansible Tower / AWX, ensuring secure access, reliable execution, and standardized usage.
 * Establishes reusable automation patterns for infrastructure provisioning, configuration management, patching, compliance, and lifecycle management.
 * Partners with Cloud, Infrastructure, and Security teams to embed automation into CI/CD pipelines, change workflows, and operational processes.
 * Provides leadership and oversight for Active Directory (on‑prem and hybrid) and Microsoft 365 (O365) automation, operational hygiene, and platform stability.
 * Ensures identity and productivity platforms meet enterprise security, compliance, and governance requirements.
 * Defines and tracks KPIs related to automation coverage, reliability, operational efficiency, and risk reduction.
 * Drives operational excellence by reducing manual intervention, improving repeatability, and increasing auditability across infrastructure platforms.
 * Evaluates new tools and technologies to improve automation maturity, standardization, and developer enablement.
 * Produces and maintains documentation, standards, and reference architectures to enable consistent execution across teams.




How we reward you:

 * Hybrid Work schedule
 * 401K with company match
 * Yearly bonus opportunity*
 * Full medical, dental, and vision insurance *
 * On-site fitness center, biometric screen, and flu shot clinic
 * Discounts at Panda restaurants, theme parks, and gym memberships
 * Paid time off starting at 15 days with 7 federal holidays*
 * Continuous education assistance and scholarships*
 * Income protection including Disability, Life and AD&D insurance*
 * Bereavement leave*
 * Benefits available for eligible permanent full time associates




Your background and experience:

 * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field required; equivalent experience may be considered
 * Minimum five years of progressive experience in infrastructure, DevOps, systems engineering, or automation roles with increasing management responsibility; demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and governing enterprise‑scale automation platforms; people leadership experience, preferably in a player‑coach capacity; strong hands‑on experience with Ansible and orchestration tooling and experience supporting hybrid infrastructure environments (on‑premises and cloud).
 * Successful completion of initial and periodically required training
 * Obtaining a valid Food Handler’s Card within 30 days of employment is a requirement of this position.




Pay Range: M2H: $128,500 - $180,000 / Annual

 * Within the range, individual pay is determined using various factors, including work location and experience.

Our Deloitte AI & Engineering team to transform technology platforms, drive innovation, and help make a significant impact on our clients' success. You'll work alongside talented professionals reimagining and reengineering operations and processes that are critical to businesses. Your contributions can help clients improve financial performance, accelerate new digital ventures, and fuel growth through innovation.

Work You'll Do

As a US Delivery Center Solution Specialist on the team, you will:

 * Design, build, and maintain secure, scalable, and automated cloud-native environments
 * Play a key role in architecting and delivering mission-critical systems and supporting both frontend and backend application development
 * Work with AWS serverless technologies, CI/CD automation, IaC, and containerized workloads


The Team

Deloitte's Government & Public Services (GPS) practice - our people, ideas, technology and outcomes - is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.

Our Engineering as a Service offering provides end-to-end design, implementation, and technology operations, leveraging our core engineering expertise. We help transform engineering teams, modernize technology, & deliver complex programs with a product engineering mindset. Our flexible delivery models- traditional teams, pools, or pods, are tailored for each client's needs, offering engineering-led Advise, Implement, & Operate capabilities to accelerate innovation.

This opportunity sits within our Deloitte US Delivery Center model, which is dedicated to driving impactful business services. It leverages Deloitte's scale and talent, as well as a center delivery model to provide high-quality, cost-effective service with standardized processes and procedures to service businesses across Deloitte.

The Deloitte US Delivery Center has a small-business feel with a big-business impact. With the resources of Deloitte and a community feel, the delivery center model provides high-quality services to our clients. USDC professionals work out of one of our specific delivery center locations, and each location presents dynamic career opportunities for professionals to focus on their work with nominal travel requirements.

Qualifications

Required:

 * Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience
 * Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future
 * 2+ years of hands-on experience with AWS DevOps
 * 2+ year's experience with CI/CD Pipeline builds (Git)
 * Must be able to obtain and maintain an Active TS Clearance and hold a minimum of an Active Secret security clearance
 * Operate under a hybrid system requiring residence within a commutable distance to Gilbert, AZ, Lake Mary, FL, or Mechanicsburg, PA or Geohubs (Atlanta, GA, Charlotte, NC, Dallas, TX, Houston, TX, or Philadelphia, PA)
 * Spend up to 30% of working time co-located at an assigned office for orchestrated opportunities, including projects, practice sessions, training, and Moments That Matter at a Deloitte Delivery Center location, Geo-Hub location, approved site, or project location
 * Maximum of 10% overnight travel for client or project purposes


Preferred:

 * Cloud native language experience (Lambda, Node.js, Python, Go)
 * API Gateway Experience
 * Exposure to cloud automation and deployment tools (CloudFormation, Ansible, Terraform, Chef)
 * Exposure to containerization (Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift, AWS EKS, AWS ECS)
